Toronto (and a few other places) use the water-equivalent of ground source heat pumps to extract heat from deep water in Lake Ontario, to harvest heat in the winter and cold in the summer. Most of downtown Toronto is cooled that way. More energy efficient than other technologies.

Just expand Deep Lake Water Cooling. It uses cold water from Lake Ontario to cool buildings in the d/t significantly reducing electricity consumption & CO2 emissions. Largest renewable system of its kind in the world, serving over 180 buildings & provides sustainable alternative to traditional AC.
